title: Tetrapogon brandegeei
taxonomy: Tetrapogon brandegeei is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Tetrapogon, which is a group of grasses commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Tetrapogon brandegeei is further classified as a member of the subfamily Chloridoideae, which contains several species of grasses with similar characteristics. The species is native to the Pacific Islands, where it can be found growing in areas with high temperatures and high humidity. This classification is based on various taxonomic studies and is widely accepted within the scientific community.
